name: C.v.woods World Championship Chili
recipeIngredient: 5 pounds Pork chops, cut into thin slices
3 Onions, medium, cut into 1/2 inch pieces
3 Green peppers, cut into 3/8 inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut chicken into pieces and combine with water in a large saucepan. Simmer for 2 hours, then strain off the broth.
2. In a 2-quart saucepan, combine celery, tomatoes, and sugar. Simmer for 1 1/2 hours. Boil chiles for 15 minutes until tender, remove seeds, and cut into 1/4-inch squares.
3. Mix oregano, cumin, MSG, pepper, salt, chili powder, cilantro, and thyme with beer until all lumps are dissolved.
4. Add the tomato mixture, chiles, and beer mixture to the chicken broth. Melt suet to make 6-8 tablespoons drippings.
5. Pour 1/3 of the suet drippings into a skillet, add half of the pork chops, and brown. Repeat with the remaining pork chops. Add the pork to the broth mixture and cook slowly for 30 minutes.
6. Trim all fat from the flank steak and cut it into 3/8-inch cubes. Brown the flank steak in the remaining drippings about a third at a time. Add it to the pork mixture. Return to simmer and cook slowly for about an hour.
7. Add onions and green peppers, simmering for 2-3 more hours, stirring every 15-20 minutes with a wooden spoon.
8. Cool for 1 hour, then refrigerate for 24 hours. Reheat the chili before serving. About 5 minutes before serving, add cheese. Just before serving, add lime juice and stir with a wooden spoon.
